title: Main concepts
description: Variables, statements, and how JS runs.

questions.prompt: Which declaration allows reassignment but has block scope?
questions.explanation: let: block-scoped and reassignable. var is function-scoped. const cannot be reassigned.

questions.prompt: Modern best practice for a value you'll never reassign:
questions.explanation: Default to const; reach for let only when reassignment is needed.
var declarations are hoisted; the value 5 isn't assigned yet — prints undefined.
let/const live in the temporal dead zone before their declaration line.

questions.prompt: Which is an EXPRESSION (returns a value)?
questions.explanation: Expressions produce values. Statements DO things (declare, loop, branch).
Reassigning a const throws TypeError: Assignment to constant variable.
const forbids REBINDING the variable — it doesn't freeze the object it points to.

questions.prompt: Which is NOT a primitive type in JS?
questions.explanation: Primitives: string, number, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, bigint. Arrays are objects.

options: Enables stricter parsing rules and helpful errors
questions.explanation: Strict mode turns silent bugs into errors. ES modules are strict by default.
